metadata.description: One of the most prominent and respected research universities in the world. A leader in global education, with more international students and more students studying abroad than any other US university. An engine of social mobility with notable representation of low-income and first-generation students among top US universities.

schools.name: College of Arts and Science (CAS)
schools.description: NYU's founding school, offering a liberal arts education for the 21st century: dynamic, practical, and purposeful. Features over 60 majors and 60 minors centered on critical engagement with ideas, writing, scientific inquiry, and data discovery.

programs.name: Computer Science
programs.detailed_description: Housed within the Courant Institute of Mathematical Sciences. Trains students in fundamental principles of computer science as well as many practical aspects of software development. 128 total credits required, with 48 credits in the major including core CS courses (Intro to CS, Data Structures, Computer Systems Org, Operating Systems, Basic Algorithms) plus 16 credits of 400-level electives. Honors program available for students with 3.65+ GPA.
learning_outcomes: Ability to write programs and design software systems
Understanding of foundational algorithms and data structures
Knowledge of computer architecture and operating systems
Deeper expertise in specific computer science domains

programs.name: Psychology
programs.detailed_description: Approaches psychology from multiple perspectives including cognitive, social, personality, neuroscience, and developmental frameworks. Emphasizes the scientific basis of psychology and encourages advanced undergraduates to engage in faculty research. 128 total credits required, 40 major credits across foundation courses, content courses (Natural Science and Social Science cores plus laboratory), and advanced electives.
learning_outcomes: Field overview and critical analysis skills
Understanding of psychology as theoretical and empirical science

programs.name: Economics
programs.detailed_description: Prepares students to understand decision-making, market structures, and global economic relationships. Emphasizes excellent student-faculty rapport with faculty engaged in active research. Two concentrations: Policy (for careers in law, public policy, business) and Theory (for PhD Economics or quantitative finance). 128 total credits required, 40 major credits.
learning_outcomes: Substantial microeconomics, macroeconomics, and econometrics knowledge
Ability to analyze problems using economic frameworks
Quantitative analysis skills with statistical models
Comprehension of modern economic research theory

programs.name: Dramatic Literature, Theatre History, and Cinema
Urban Studies and Social and Cultural Analysis

schools.name: Leonard N. Stern School of Business
schools.description: One of the first business schools in the United States, founded in 1900. Emphasizes a first-rate undergraduate business education grounded in five pillars: academics, social impact, global engagement, professionalism, and community. AACSB accredited. STEM-certified BS degree integrating business with liberal arts.

programs.name: Business
programs.detailed_description: STEM-certified degree integrating business education with liberal arts grounding. Curriculum emphasizes global perspectives, social responsibility, and professional ethics. 128 total credits. 12 available concentrations: Accounting, Actuarial Science, Computing and Data Science, Business Economics, Econometrics and Quantitative Economics, Finance, Management and Organizations, Marketing, Operations, Real Estate, Statistics, and Sustainable Business. Entrepreneurship available as co-concentration.

schools.name: Tisch School of the Arts
schools.description: Has provided training for artists and scholars for over 50 years, leveraging NYU's resources and New York City's cultural environment. Features state-of-the-art facilities, a collaborative community of award-winning faculty members, and internship opportunities with established professionals.

programs.name: Film and Television
programs.detailed_description: The Kanbar Institute of Film and Television has been a major cultural force since 1965. Notable graduates include directors Chris Columbus, Ryan Fleck, Nia DaCosta; cinematographer Rachel Morrison; and TV creators Vince Gilligan and Damon Lindelof. 128 total credits required: 63-66 major credits (production safety, history/criticism, core production, craft, screenwriting), 44-48 general education credits, and 30 elective credits.

schools.name: Tandon School of Engineering
schools.description: Home to a vibrant community of unconventional engineers focused on technology that powers cities, enables worldwide communication, fights climate change. Dates to 1854, located in downtown Brooklyn.

programs.name: Computer Science
programs.detailed_description: Focuses on designing and building computer systems, from smart phones to complex databases. Offers specialized research labs in cybersecurity, game engineering, and big data analysis. 128 total credits: 38 credits core CS (Intro to Programming, Data Structures and Algorithms, OOP, Computer Architecture, Operating Systems, Design & Analysis of Algorithms, Software Engineering, Design Project), 16 math credits, 13-16 science/engineering credits, 20 humanities/social sciences credits, 51 elective credits.

schools.name: Steinhardt School of Culture, Education, and Human Development
schools.description: Established in 1890 as the School of Pedagogy. Offers a broad array of degrees and programs, all designed to advance the education, health, and well-being of people and communities around the world.

programs.name: Applied Psychology
programs.detailed_description: Interdisciplinary program teaching students to bridge psychological theory, research, and practice to solve everyday problems in a multicultural world. Focuses on learning development, cultural competency, public health, and mental wellness. 128 total credits: 56 general education, 28 psychology core, 12 research/statistics, 10 field experiences, 22 electives. STEM OPT eligible for F-1 students.

programs.name: Early Childhood Education/Early Childhood Special Education
Global Public Health/Communicative Sciences and Disorders
Global Public Health/Media, Culture, and Communication

schools.name: Silver School of Social Work
schools.description: Founded in 1960, a premier locus for research and education of professional social workers. Has trained over 20,000 practitioners. Emphasizes clinical excellence, social justice commitment, and innovative teaching methods focused on complex urban environments.

programs.name: Global Public Health and Social Work

schools.name: Gallatin School of Individualized Study
schools.description: Provides a distinctive liberal arts education for a diverse student body emphasizing self-directed, interdisciplinary learning. Founded in 1972 as an experimental program, gained official school status in 1995. Has graduated over 9,800 students. Students create customized majors across traditional academic disciplines with faculty guidance.

schools.name: School of Professional Studies (SPS)
schools.description: An internationally recognized institution that transforms today's students into tomorrow's changemakers and industry leaders. Operating since 1934, emphasizes experiential learning across fields including real estate, hospitality, sports business, and integrated marketing.

schools.name: Liberal Studies
schools.description: Home to NYU's second-largest population of undergraduates. Founded in 2009, distinguished by small classes and an environment in which faculty and students work closely together to develop a community dedicated to critical inquiry, research, and exploration. A vibrant liberal arts college environment within the framework of a major global research university.

programs.name: Liberal Studies Core
programs.description: A two-year interdisciplinary global curriculum from which students directly transition to 90 majors at NYU to complete their bachelor's degree

schools.name: Rory Meyers College of Nursing
schools.description: Founded in 1932, the second-largest private university college of nursing in the US. Generates knowledge through nursing research, educates nursing leaders, and shapes future healthcare policy. Home to the oldest PhD in nursing program in the country.

schools.name: School of Global Public Health
schools.description: Offers thirteen innovative combined majors in collaboration with other prestigious schools. All majors require 6 core public health courses (24 credits), 1-3 combined major electives, 90 hours of internship experience, and a semester-long study abroad component at an NYU global site.

programs.name: Note: Programs are listed under partner schools (CAS, Steinhardt, Silver, Nursing, Liberal Studies)
programs.description: 13 combined majors pairing Global Public Health with disciplines across NYU schools

schools.name: NYU Abu Dhabi
schools.description: Abu Dhabi's first American-style, comprehensive, liberal arts and sciences research university. Operates as a force for social and educational progress and intercultural understanding.

schools.name: NYU Shanghai
schools.description: China's first Sino-US research university and the third degree-granting campus of the NYU Global Network, established in 2012. Enrolls approximately 2,000 students, with half from China and half from other nations globally.
